content.text: `__ieee754_acos` is **not** an entry function in this codebase. The actual entry point is `acos` (and `__gf_dpacos`) defined in `s_acos.s`. The `__ieee754_acos` symbol in `e_acos.s` is a separate assembly routine - it's a lower-level IEEE754 kernel, but there are no C callers referencing it (the `w_acos.c` wrapper that would normally call it does not exist here). The `acos` entry in `s_acos.s` contains the full implementation inline rather than delegating to `__ieee754_acos`.

content.text:
Nobody calls `__ieee754_acos` - it's defined in `e_acos.s` but never referenced anywhere else in the codebase. It appears to be dead code, likely a leftover from the original Newlib/Andes transliteration where the `e_` (error-handling wrapper) and `s_` (entry) functions were separate. In this RISC-V port, `s_acos.s` contains the full implementation inline and makes `e_acos.s` unreachable.

The contrast with `__ieee754_acosh` in `e_acosh.s` is worth checking - that one may actually be called.

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)

  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

content.content:
# Mathematical Functions Documentation - Complete List

## Category 1: Trigonometric Functions
- `sin`, `sinf` — sine
- `cos`, `cosf` — cosine
- `tan`, `tanf` — tangent
- `asin`, `asinf` — arc sine
- `acos`, `acosf` — arc cosine
- `atan`, `atanf` — arc tangent
- `atan2`, `atan2f` — arc tangent of y/x

## Category 2: Hyperbolic Functions
- `sinh`, `sinhf` — hyperbolic sine
- `cosh`, `coshf` — hyperbolic cosine
- `tanh`, `tanhf` — hyperbolic tangent
- `asinh`, `asinhf` — inverse hyperbolic sine
- `acosh`, `acoshf` — inverse hyperbolic cosine
- `atanh`, `atanhf` — inverse hyperbolic tangent

## Category 3: Exponential & Logarithmic Functions
- `exp`, `expf` — exponential (base e)
- `exp2`, `exp2f` — exponential (base 2)
- `exp10`, `exp10f` — exponential (base 10)
- `expm1`, `expm1f` — exponential minus 1
- `log`, `logf` — natural logarithm
- `log10`, `log10f` — base 10 logarithm
- `log2`, `log2f` — base 2 logarithm
- `log1p`, `log1pf` — logarithm of 1 + x
- `pow`, `powf` — x to power y
- `pow10`, `pow10f` — base 10 power

## Category 4: Root & Power Functions
- `sqrt`, `sqrtf` — positive square root
- `cbrt`, `cbrtf` — cube root
- `hypot`, `hypotf` — distance from origin

## Category 5: Rounding Functions
- `floor`, `floorf` — round downward
- `ceil`, `ceilf` — round upward
- `round`, `roundf` — round to nearest
- `trunc`, `truncf` — round toward zero
- `rint`, `rintf` — round to integer
- `nearbyint`, `nearbyintf` — round to integer (no exception)
- `lrint`, `lrintf`, `llrint`, `llrintf` — round to long integer
- `lround`, `lroundf`, `llround`, `llroundf` — round to nearest long

## Category 6: Remainder & Modulo Functions
- `fmod`, `fmodf` — floating-point remainder
- `remainder`, `remainderf` — round and remainder
- `remquo`, `remquof` — remainder and quotient

## Category 7: Floating-Point Manipulation
- `frexp`, `frexpf` — split into mantissa and exponent
- `ldexp`, `ldexpf` — multiply by power of 2
- `modf`, `modff` — split fractional and integer parts
- `scalbn`, `scalbnf`, `scalbln`, `scalblnf` — scale by FLT_RADIX power
- `ilogb`, `ilogbf` — extract exponent
- `logb`, `logbf` — extract exponent (floating-point)
- `nextafter`, `nextafterf` — next representable value
- `copysign`, `copysignf` — copy sign between values

## Category 8: Extremum Functions
- `fmax`, `fmaxf` — maximum value
- `fmin`, `fminf` — minimum value
- `fdim`, `fdimf` — positive difference
- `fma`, `fmaf` — fused multiply-add

## Category 9: Absolute Value
- `fabs`, `fabsf` — absolute value (magnitude)

## Category 10: Special Value Generation
- `infinity`, `infinityf` — IEEE infinity representation
- `nan`, `nanf` — NaN (not-a-number) representation

## Category 11: Classification & Comparison Macros
- `fpclassify` — classify floating-point value
- `isfinite` — test for finite value
- `isinf` — test for infinity
- `isnan` — test for NaN
- `isnormal` — test for normal value
- `isgreater` — greater-than comparison
- `isgreaterequal` — greater-than-or-equal comparison
- `isless` — less-than comparison
- `islessequal` — less-than-or-equal comparison
- `islessgreater` — less-than-or-greater comparison
- `isunordered` — test for unordered relationship
- `signbit` — test for negative sign
- `finite`, `finitef` — archaic finite test
- `isinf`, `isinff` — archaic infinity test
- `isnan`, `isnanf` — archaic NaN test

## Category 12: Error & Special Functions
- `erf`, `erff` — error function
- `erfc`, `erfcf` — complementary error function
- `gamma`, `gammaf` — logarithmic gamma (archaic)
- `lgamma`, `lgammaf` — logarithmic gamma
- `tgamma`, `tgammaf` — true gamma function
- `gamma_r`, `gammaf_r`, `lgamma_r`, `lgammaf_r` — reentrant gamma variants

## Category 13: Bessel Functions
- `j0`, `j0f` — Bessel function of first kind, order 0
- `j1`, `j1f` — Bessel function of first kind, order 1
- `jn`, `jnf` — Bessel function of first kind, order n
- `y0`, `y0f` — Bessel function of second kind, order 0
- `y1`, `y1f` — Bessel function of second kind, order 1
- `yn`, `ynf` — Bessel function of second kind, order n

## Complex Number Functions (complex.h)

### Complex Trigonometric/Hyperbolic
- `csin`, `csinf` — complex sine
- `ccos`, `ccosf` — complex cosine
- `ctan`, `ctanf` — complex tangent
- `csinh`, `csinhf` — complex hyperbolic sine
- `ccosh`, `ccoshf` — complex hyperbolic cosine
- `ctanh`, `ctanhf` — complex hyperbolic tangent

### Complex Inverse Trigonometric/Hyperbolic
- `casin`, `casinf` — complex arc sine
- `cacos`, `cacosf` — complex arc cosine
- `catan`, `catanf` — complex arc tangent
- `casinh`, `casinhf` — complex arc hyperbolic sine
- `cacosh`, `cacoshf` — complex arc hyperbolic cosine
- `catanh`, `catanhf` — complex arc hyperbolic tangent

### Complex Exponential & Logarithmic
- `cexp`, `cexpf` — complex exponential
- `clog`, `clogf` — complex natural logarithm
- `clog10`, `clog10f` — complex base-10 logarithm
- `cpow`, `cpowf` — complex power

### Complex Utility
- `cabs`, `cabsf`, `cabsl` — complex absolute value
- `carg`, `cargf` — complex argument (phase angle)
- `creal`, `crealf`, `creall` — extract real part
- `cimag`, `cimagf`, `cimagl` — extract imaginary part
- `conj`, `conjf` — complex conjugate
- `csqrt`, `csqrtf` — complex square root
- `cproj`, `cprojf` — Riemann sphere projection

## Floating-Point Environment Functions (fenv.h)

- `feclearexcept` — clear exceptions
- `fegetenv` — retrieve environment
- `fegetexceptflag` — get status flags
- `fegetround` — get rounding direction
- `feholdexcept` — save environment
- `feraiseexcept` — raise exceptions
- `fesetenv` — set environment
- `fesetexceptflag` — set status flags
- `fesetround` — set rounding direction
- `fetestexcept` — test exception flags
- `feupdateenv` — update environment

# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
